experiment: #8 hard-config curriculum (prioritized resets) on in-loop residual
box: b3060 (vast 41649155), 4x RTX3060 12GB, SHARED (foreign tenant on GPU2/3); used GPU0/1
metric: real success = max over 1000-step rollout of metrics[box_target]>=0.9 (grasp-gated); n=256; evaluated on UNIFORM held-out config distribution (RES_CURRICULUM unset at eval) for an honest number. PEAK + FINAL reported.

curriculum_mechanism.design: per-env ring buffer (K=64) of recently-FAILED (box0,target) configs threaded through brax AutoResetWrapper_preserve_info; full_reset=True; ~50%% resets replay a failed config + ~50%% uniform. Selection+box-replacement at first step of each episode (is_new) because the wrapper hides preserve_info from reset(). Failure = episode max box_target<0.9.
curriculum_mechanism.in_loop_residual: inherited verbatim (alpha=1.0, phase-z Markov obs, milestone shaping); shared residual_env.py NOT edited

training_config: brax PPO official manipulation_params PandaPickCube branch: num_envs=2048, episode_length=150, unroll=10, lr=1e-3, entropy=2e-2, discounting=0.97, num_minibatches=32, batch=512, num_evals=21. num_timesteps target=40M; brax step counter overshoots to 65.5M (ceil rounding + num_resets_per_eval). alpha fixed=1.0.
verdict: NO (strong negative). Hard-config curriculum does NOT lift uniform-eval real success above 0.79; it CATASTROPHICALLY COLLAPSES it: control (no curriculum) peak 0.79/0.80 (mean 0.795, reproduces the 0.79 bar), curriculum peak 0.12/0.09 (mean 0.103). Curriculum training reward also depressed (~850 vs control ~1200) and learns slower. The curriculum policy still REACHES the box (reached~0.97-1.0) but fails the lift-to-target on uniform configs (low mbt) -> overfits the hard-config tail / destabilizes the in-loop residual.
confound_honest_note: The control used full_reset=False (cached per-env config, the stock residual training regime), while the curriculum REQUIRED full_reset=True (re-reset per episode) for prioritized resets. So this A/B conflates the prioritized-failed sampling with the full_reset training regime. A cleaner isolation would add a third arm: full_reset=True + UNIFORM resets (no replay). As implemented, the prioritized-reset curriculum is a clear net loss; whether full_reset alone or the hard-config oversampling is the dominant cause is not isolated here. Either way, the curriculum-as-designed does not help.
honesty_note: all numbers from real rollouts written to per-run JSON (eval_*.json); eval on UNIFORM held-out distribution (RES_CURRICULUM unset) so the curriculum distribution cannot game the reported number. n=256, 1000-step, alpha=1.0.
